meta.name: Lexy AI — Conversation Flow Registry
meta.description: Static JSON registry powering the Lexy AI pipeline viewer, API response layer, and dashboard renderer. Each entry represents one user question turn — including intent classification, signal extraction, concept mapping, schema retrieval, metric resolution, and final output assembly. Supports dashboard, inline analysis, alert, and follow-up question types.

conversations.user_question: We have a SOC2 audit in 30 days. Our security compliance training rate is at 71%. We need to get to 90%. What's the gap and how do we close it?

signals.value: compliance_rate — current 71%, target 90%, gap 19pts immediately computable
30-day hard deadline — lag structure analysis required before any recommendation
Gap Analysis with forward projection — intervention ordering is critical
SOC2 audit — audit posture (trajectory + evidence) matters as much as raw rate
Is this fixable in 30 days? Answer requires causal lag structure, not just current rate

implicit_questions: Can we realistically close 19 points in 30 days?
Is the shortfall caused by disengagement or assignment overload?
What is a defensible audit posture if we can't hit 90%?

concepts.name: Compliance Training
concepts.description: Primary — compliance_rate is the terminal metric. SOC2 security training is the specific program type.

concepts.name: Learner Operations
concepts.description: Assignment distribution and overdue queue management are the operational levers behind the compliance terminal.

concepts.name: Deadline & SLA Tracking
concepts.description: SOC2 requires time-bounded completion — deadline proximity and causal lag structure determine what is achievable in 30 days.

concepts.name: Audit Evidence
concepts.description: Audit posture — trajectory and documented corrective action matter as much as point-in-time rate for SOC2 auditors.

excluded.name: ilt_instructor_scheduling
excluded.reason: ILT capacity not primary for SOC2 security training — web-based course format confirmed

excluded.name: vendor_roi_analysis
excluded.reason: Cost/ROI not relevant to a deadline-driven gap close

excluded.name: manager_effectiveness
excluded.reason: Manager activation is a downstream tactic, not a concept area for this gap analysis

areas.name: Overdue & Deadlines
areas.why: Key mediator in 28-day window — overdue_count → missed_deadline → compliance_rate

areas.name: Assignment Distribution
areas.why: Root cause — compliance_assigned_distribution controls queue depth

areas.name: Learner / Employee
areas.why: Denominator — lm_learner_total is a confounder on department comparisons; size-adjustment required

areas.name: Engagement Signals
areas.why: login_count_weekly_trend distinguishes overload from disengagement — changes intervention type

areas.name: ILT Instructor Data
areas.why: Checked — not applicable for web-based SOC2 courses

stage_4_metric_resolution.drop_reason: trend metrics without target_value, invalid for goal-bounded gap analysis

collider_warnings.warning: COLLIDER — driven simultaneously by assignment_pressure AND genuine engagement. Spurious cross-department comparisons if used as primary. All Shapley computed against compliance_rate (terminal).

recommended_metrics.name: compliance_rate
recommended_metrics.why: Terminal — current 71%, target 90%, gap 19pts. All Shapley attribution computed against this.

recommended_metrics.name: overdue_count
recommended_metrics.why: Primary mediator — ϕ=0.58 of gap. Direct intervention target. 28d total path reachable.

recommended_metrics.name: missed_deadline_count
recommended_metrics.why: Second mediator in chain. overdue→missed→compliance. 28d total lag — just within window.

recommended_metrics.name: login_count_weekly_trend
recommended_metrics.why: Root signal — STABLE here. Rules out disengagement. Blocks reminder email recommendation.

recommended_metrics.name: compliance_assigned_distribution
recommended_metrics.why: Root cause (Oct assignment batch) — full chain fix takes 42d. Outside 30d window.

recommended_metrics.name: completion_rate
recommended_metrics.why: COLLIDER — blocked as primary metric. Caused by assignment_pressure AND engagement simultaneously.

agent_flow.narrative: Identified as compliance_gap_close (0.97). Focus: compliance_training, learner_operations. Requires deadline dimension and causal lag structure.
7-step plan. Gap analyzer primary. CCE required in causal-path mode to determine which lag chains are reachable within 30d window.
Retrieved 6 schemas and 22 candidate metrics across compliance, assignment, and engagement domains.
agent_flow.detail: schemas=6 | candidates=22 | priority: compliance_status, overdue_assignments, login_events

agent_flow.narrative: Qualified 22 → 14. Dropped 8 trend metrics without target_value. Collider flag raised on completion_rate. Deadline dimension enforced.
agent_flow.detail: dt_scored=14 | dropped=8 (trend, no target) | collider_flagged=[completion_rate] | groups: compliance_terminal(1), mediators(2), root_signals(2)

agent_flow.narrative: Building causal paths from root signals to compliance_rate. Timing each path against 30d window. assignment→compliance = 42d (outside). overdue→compliance = 28d (inside).
Computing 19pt gap. Running Shapley against compliance_rate terminal. ϕ_overdue=0.58. login_trend=stable — rules out disengagement hypothesis.
agent_flow.detail: gap=19pts | shapley: ϕ_overdue=0.58, ϕ_assignment=0.31 | disengagement_hypothesis=REJECTED (login stable)

summary.what: compliance_rate at 71% against a 90% SOC2 target — 19pt gap with 30 days remaining. Engineering 58% (89 overdue), Product 64% (41 overdue), Sales 73% (28 overdue). Engineering + Product = 71% of overdue queue despite 38% of headcount.
summary.why: Root cause is assignment queue overload, not disengagement. login_count_weekly_trend is STABLE (disengagement hypothesis rejected). Shapley ϕ_overdue = 0.58. The full causal chain (assignment distribution → compliance_rate) takes 42 days — outside the 30d window. Only overdue_count → missed_deadline → compliance_rate (28d lag) is reachable.
summary.insight: Without intervention: Engineering ~64%, Product ~69% at audit date — both below the 80% SOC2 technical-staff threshold. With direct outreach (247 overdue learners) + manager activation: Engineering 79–82%, Product 83–86%. Full 90% in 30 days is unlikely. 84% with documented corrective action is a defensible SOC2 audit posture — auditors evaluate trajectory, not just point-in-time rate.
summary.intervention_recommendation: Direct outreach to 247 overdue learners. Manager activation for Engineering (89) and Product (41) cohorts. Do NOT send blanket reminder emails — login is stable, adding email volume to overloaded queue is counterproductive (expected negative impact).

charts.title: Shapley Attribution ϕ — Causal Drivers of Gap

outcomes.question: Can we close 19pts in 30 days?
outcomes.answer: Partially. Reach 84–87% with full intervention — not 90%. The full root-cause chain (assignment distribution → compliance) takes 42 days. Only the overdue path (28d) is within window.

outcomes.question: Is this a motivation problem? Send reminder emails?
outcomes.answer: No — and no. login_count_weekly_trend is stable. Root cause is assignment queue overload (ϕ=0.58). Reminder emails add volume to overwhelmed learners. Expected impact: negative.

outcomes.question: Which teams are most critical?
outcomes.answer: Engineering (58%, −32pts, 89 overdue) and Product (64%, −26pts, 41 overdue). Both below the 80% SOC2 technical-staff threshold.

outcomes.question: What's a defensible audit posture?
outcomes.answer: 84% with documented corrective action — escalation plan, manager activation records, and remediation timeline. SOC2 auditors evaluate trajectory + evidence, not just point-in-time rate.

followup_suggestions: Break the compliance gap down by department for me
Which employees are at highest risk of missing the deadline?
What intervention should I run first — outreach or manager activation?
Build me a compliance tracking dashboard for the L&D director

department — extracted from 'by department' phrasing
compliance_completion_rate — already qualified in conv_001 state

followup_callout.description: Running after the Gap Analysis (conv_001). dt_scored_metrics (14) and csod_resolved_schemas (6) are already in state. Follow-up router detected this and bypassed 6 spine steps.

agent_flow.narrative: Turn 2 detected. dt_scored_metrics present (14 metrics). Schemas present (6 tables). Question matches cohort_comparator capability. Routing directly.
agent_flow.detail: is_followup=true | executor_id=cohort_comparator | skipped=[intent_classifier, planner, mdl_retrieval, metrics_retrieval, scoring_validator, dt_resolver]

agent_flow.narrative: Segmenting by department using workday_employees join. Computing compliance_completion_rate per cohort. Identifying statistical outliers from company mean.
Assembling cohort comparison report alongside existing gap_report artifacts from conv_001.

summary.what: Range: Legal 94% → Engineering 58%. Company mean: 73.2%. 4 departments above target, 7 below. Engineering, Product, and Operations are more than 1.5σ below the company mean.
summary.why: Engineering and Product carry the bulk of the overdue queue (71% of overdue items, 38% of headcount) — established in gap analysis. Operations gap is separate: driven by high ILT dependency and scheduling constraints.
summary.insight: The 36-point spread between Legal and Engineering is unusually wide. Median industry spread for similar-size companies is ~18 points. Sales improved +11.2 points since last quarter — linked to manager accountability program launched in October. That intervention model is directly transferable to Engineering.

charts.title: Compliance Rate by Department — All 11 Cohorts

outcomes.question: How does compliance compare across all 11 departments?
outcomes.answer: Range: Legal 94% → Engineering 58%. Company mean: 73.2%. 4 departments above target, 7 below.

outcomes.question: Which department improved most since last quarter?
outcomes.answer: Sales improved +11.2 points — from 65% to 76.2%. Attributed to manager accountability program launched in October.

outcomes.question: Which departments are statistically below the company mean?
outcomes.answer: Engineering (58%), Product (61%), and Operations (64%) — all more than 1.5σ below the mean. 3 departments flagged.

outcomes.question: What is the spread between best and worst performing?
outcomes.answer: 36 percentage points separating Legal (94%) from Engineering (58%). Unusually wide — median industry spread is ~18 points.

followup_suggestions: Who on the Engineering team is at highest risk of missing the deadline?
Show me the week-by-week trend for Engineering specifically
Generate a team training dashboard for the Procurement division

conversations.user_question: Build a compliance training dashboard for our L&D director showing this quarter's performance.

signals.value: l&d_director — determines metric depth and layout template selection
dashboard — explicit build request
this quarter — Q4 FY26
compliance_training + ld_operations — dual focus confirmed

implicit_questions: What is our overall completion rate?
Which departments need L&D director attention?
What is the certification renewal pipeline?
How is budget tracking against allocation?

concepts.name: Compliance Training
concepts.description: Primary — compliance_rate and cert_compliance_rate are the L&D director's headline KPIs.

concepts.name: Trend Analysis
concepts.description: L&D director needs week-over-week trend to communicate trajectory to leadership.

concepts.name: Budget & Cost
concepts.description: L&D director is accountable for training spend vs allocation — must be in executive summary.

concepts.name: Cert Renewal Pipeline
concepts.description: Upcoming cert expirations are an operational priority that falls under L&D oversight.

excluded.name: individual_learner_transcript
excluded.reason: L&D director persona — aggregate view, not individual drill-down

excluded.name: vendor_contract_details
excluded.reason: Below L&D director scope for a QBR-level dashboard

stage_4_metric_resolution.drop_reason: low DT score for l&d_director persona; granular operational metrics below director scope

agent_flow.narrative: Identified as dashboard_generation_for_persona (0.95). Persona: l&d_director. Strong signals: 'build dashboard', 'L&D director'. Focus: compliance_training, ld_operations.
8-step plan. CCE runs in centrality mode first. Layout resolver reads centrality to tag leading/lagging. Dashboard generator reads finalised layout.
Retrieved 9 schemas and 19 candidate metrics for l&d_director persona (strategic + operational mix).
Resolving l&d_director audience level. Groups formed for executive summary, trend analysis, and operational detail sections.
agent_flow.detail: retained=13 | groups: executive_kpis(4), trend_charts(5), operational_detail(4) | persona_scoped=l&d_director

agent_flow.narrative: Building metric relationship graph. Computing centrality from topology. Leading indicators (high out_degree) tagged for primary placement. Written to csod_causal_centrality.
agent_flow.detail: nodes=13 | edges=17 | leading: completion_rate(out=5), login_freq(out=4) | lagging: pass_rate(in=4) | written to csod_causal_centrality

agent_flow.narrative: Mapping 3 DT groups → dashboard sections. Reading centrality to place leading metrics first. Writing finalised layout to csod_dt_layout.
agent_flow.detail: sections=[top_kpis(4), trends_row(4 charts), detail_tables(3)] | leading_first=true | written to csod_dt_layout

agent_flow.narrative: Building 11 components from csod_dt_layout. Leading/lagging tags sourced from centrality annotations. Output: compliance L&D director dashboard.

summary.what: 78.4% compliance completion (↑4.2% QoQ). Target: 90%. Gap: 11.6 points. On current trajectory (+1.8%/week): will reach 85% by quarter end — still below 90% target. Engineering 58%, Operations 61%, Product 64% need immediate L&D director attention.
summary.why: Steady weekly improvement reflects manager accountability program launched in October. Cert renewal pipeline is at risk: 77 of 234 expiring certs (33%) have not started renewal.
summary.insight: At +1.8%/week, the program needs to maintain cadence for 6+ more weeks to reach 90% — this extends past quarter end. L&D director should escalate the 3 critical departments and formalize corrective action documentation for audit trail.

outcomes.question: What is our overall compliance training completion rate?
outcomes.answer: 78.4% — ↑4.2% vs last quarter. Target: 90%. Gap: 11.6 points.

outcomes.question: Which 3 departments need immediate L&D attention?
outcomes.answer: Engineering 58%, Operations 61%, Product 64% — all below 65% and trending flat or declining.

outcomes.question: How is completion trending week-over-week?
outcomes.answer: +1.8%/week over 8 consecutive weeks. On current trajectory: will reach 85% by quarter end — still below 90% target.

outcomes.question: What is the certification renewal pipeline for the next 30 days?
outcomes.answer: 234 certifications expiring in 30 days. 157 (67%) renewal in progress. 77 not yet started. Action required.

outcomes.question: How is training spend tracking vs budget?
outcomes.answer: $187K spent of $240K budget — 78% utilisation at Q4 week 10. On track. Largest spend: vendor-led ILT ($82K).

followup_suggestions: Show me which employees haven't started their cert renewal
What's driving the Engineering gap specifically?
Forecast what our compliance rate will be by audit date

conversations.user_question: Who's going to miss our SOC2 training deadline next Friday?

signals.value: next Friday — 7-day risk window, deadline-bounded
all active learners — full roster scan required
named at-risk learner list — learner-level prediction
implied — user wants to act, not just observe

implicit_questions: Which manager owns the most at-risk learners?
What's the expected completion rate if we act today?

concepts.name: Compliance Risk
concepts.description: Primary — risk_of_non_compliance is the terminal prediction target with 7-day horizon.

concepts.name: Learner Behaviour
concepts.description: Login recency, progress percentage, and session history are the primary risk signals per learner.

concepts.name: Manager Hierarchy
concepts.description: Manager ownership of at-risk learners determines escalation routing.

excluded.name: course_content_quality
excluded.reason: Content feedback not relevant to 7-day deadline risk — no time to redesign content

excluded.name: historical_roi
excluded.reason: ROI analysis irrelevant to a deadline-bounded intervention question

stage_4_metric_resolution.drop_reason: requires_deadline_dimension enforced — dropped 4 metrics without due_date or expiry columns

agent_flow.narrative: Identified as predictive_risk_analysis (0.93). Signals: 'going to miss', 'deadline', 'next Friday'. Focus: compliance_training. Deadline dimension required.
7-step plan. DT enforces deadline dimension. CCE required for causal edge structure. Risk predictor runs Shapley per-learner against compliance_posture goal.
Retrieved 6 schemas. Priority: training_assignments (due_date ✓), workday_employees (manager_hierarchy ✓), sessions (login_recency ✓).
Enforcing requires_deadline_dimension. Dropped 4 metrics without due_date or expiry columns. 7 risk indicator metrics retained.
agent_flow.detail: retained=7 | dropped=4 (no deadline col) | groups: compliance_urgency(4), engagement_signals(3)

agent_flow.narrative: Mapping causal edges between compliance indicators. Edges used as coalition structure for risk predictor's Shapley computation.
agent_flow.detail: nodes=14 | edges=12 | top: days_remaining→compliance(0.38), progress_pct→compliance(0.29) | written to csod_causal_edges

agent_flow.narrative: Scoring 1,847 active learners. Shapley attribution per-learner vs compliance_posture goal. high_risk=127, medium_risk=89.
agent_flow.detail: learners_scored=1847 | shapley: per-learner vs compliance_posture | high_risk=127 | medium_risk=89

summary.what: 127 high-risk employees (likely to miss next Friday's SOC2 deadline). 89 medium-risk (may miss without intervention). Total exposure: 216 of 1,847 active learners. Sarah Chen's team has 14 high-risk learners (64% of her team). Primary bottleneck: SOC2 Access Control module — 43% of high-risk learners stuck here with < 20% completion.
summary.why: Risk concentration driven by three factors: days_remaining (ϕ=0.38), progress_pct below 30% (ϕ=0.29), and login recency over 14 days (ϕ=0.19). These are learners who haven't engaged, not learners who are disengaged — the module itself has an 8-minute average dropout.
summary.insight: If interventions are executed today: estimated 91.3% completion by Friday (vs 82.4% with no action) — +8.9 point uplift. Assumes 60% manager notification response rate. High-risk intervention: calendar block + manager notification + direct module link. Medium-risk: in-app nudge with suggested 2-hour time slot.

outcomes.question: How many employees will miss the SOC2 deadline?
outcomes.answer: 127 high-risk (likely to miss). 89 medium-risk (may miss without intervention). Total exposure: 216 of 1,847 active learners.

outcomes.question: Which manager owns the most at-risk learners?
outcomes.answer: Sarah Chen's team — 14 high-risk out of 22 total team members (64%). Recommended: immediate manager notification with team-level dashboard.

outcomes.question: What course is the single biggest bottleneck?
outcomes.answer: SOC2 Access Control module — 43% of high-risk learners stuck here with < 20% completion. Average time in module before dropout: 8 minutes.

outcomes.question: What intervention is recommended?
outcomes.answer: High-risk: Automated calendar block + manager notification + direct module link. Medium-risk: In-app nudge with suggested 2-hour time slot.

outcomes.question: Expected completion rate if we act today?
outcomes.answer: Estimated 91.3% by Friday (vs 82.4% with no action). Intervention uplift: +8.9 points. Assumes 60% response rate on manager notifications.

followup_suggestions: Send me the list of 127 high-risk learners with their manager names
Draft the manager notification message for Sarah Chen's team
Show me the Training Plan dashboard for Procurement

conversations.user_question: [SYSTEM ALERT] Anomaly detected in learner engagement data. login_count_weekly has dropped 3.2σ below 90-day mean. Triggered: Nov 14. Escalating to Lexy for root cause analysis.

signals.value: system_alert — auto-escalated from monitoring layer, not user-initiated
login_count_weekly — z = -3.2 below 90-day mean
trend_only — HARD constraint, all current_state metrics dropped
upstream_trace + downstream_propagation — distinguish pipeline error from business signal

concepts.name: Learner Engagement
concepts.description: Primary — login_count_weekly is a leading engagement signal. Trend analysis only.

concepts.name: Platform Health
concepts.description: Upstream trace required — must rule out ETL or ingestion failure before treating as business signal.

concepts.name: Propagation Lag
concepts.description: Downstream walk needed — estimate when completion_rate and assessment_attempts will be impacted.

excluded.name: current_state_kpis
excluded.reason: HARD constraint: enforce_trend_only=TRUE for anomaly detection. All current_state metrics dropped.

stage_4_metric_resolution.drop_reason: enforce_trend_only=TRUE — all current_state metrics dropped, only timestamped trend metrics retained

agent_flow.narrative: System-triggered anomaly alert. Identified as anomaly_detection (0.98). HARD constraint: enforce_trend_only=TRUE. CCE in upstream/downstream traversal mode.
6-step plan. DT hard-drops all current_state. CCE walks edges upstream (ETL check) and downstream (propagation estimate). Anomaly detector applies Z-score.
Retrieved 5 schemas with timestamp columns. 18 candidate metrics across engagement and platform health.
agent_flow.detail: schemas=5 (timestamp ✓ all) | candidates=18 | focus: engagement_trends, platform_health

agent_flow.narrative: Hard-dropping all current_state (enforce_trend_only). 18 → 7 trend metrics retained.
agent_flow.detail: retained=7 | dropped=11 (current_state — invalid for anomaly detection)

agent_flow.narrative: Upstream walk: checking etl_session_counts and app_push_events for simultaneous anomaly. Downstream walk: estimating propagation to completion_rate and assessment_attempts.
agent_flow.detail: nodes=7 | edges=11 | upstream: no anomaly in ETL or push → primary signal confirmed | downstream: completion_rate lag ~12d, assessment lag ~8d | written to csod_causal_edges

agent_flow.narrative: Z-score across 7 time-series (90d window). login_frequency: Nov 14, z=-3.2. Upstream walk: no upstream anomaly → real business signal (not pipeline error). Downstream: completion_rate impacted in ~12 days.
agent_flow.detail: method=zscore | window=90d | threshold=2.0σ | anomalies=2 | primary: login_frequency (z=-3.2) | upstream=clean | downstream_lag: completion_rate=12d, assessment=8d

alert_card.root_cause: Q4 performance review cycle — historical pattern (Q4 2022, Q4 2023)

summary.what: Login frequency dropped to 3.2σ below the 90-day mean starting November 14th (18 days ago). Raw session table counts confirm the drop is genuine — not an ingestion artifact.
summary.why: Correlates with Q4 performance review cycle — login frequency dropped 34% during review weeks. This pattern is historically recurring (Q4 2022: −28%, Q4 2023: −31%). Performance review periods consistently suppress LMS engagement.
summary.insight: Completion rate will be impacted in ~12 days. Assessment attempts in ~8 days. Both are currently within normal range but trending toward anomaly threshold. Recommend proactive communication to remind learners of outstanding assignments before downstream metrics cross the alert threshold.

charts.title: Login Frequency — 90-Day Trend with Anomaly Window

outcomes.question: When did the anomaly start and how severe?
outcomes.answer: November 14th — 18 days ago. Login frequency dropped to 3.2σ below the 90-day mean. Severity: high.

outcomes.question: Is this a pipeline error or a real business signal?
outcomes.answer: Real business signal. ETL logs show no gaps or failures. Raw session counts confirm the drop is genuine.

outcomes.question: What caused the engagement drop?
outcomes.answer: Q4 performance review cycle — login frequency historically drops 28–34% during review weeks. Pattern confirmed in Q4 2022 and Q4 2023.

outcomes.question: Which metrics will this propagate to, and when?
outcomes.answer: Completion rate: impacted in ~12 days. Assessment attempts: ~8 days. Both approaching anomaly threshold.

followup_suggestions: Which departments are most affected by the engagement drop?
Draft a reminder email to learners with outstanding assignments
Show me historical Q4 engagement patterns

agent_flow.narrative: Identified as training_plan_dashboard (0.89). Persona: hr_bp. Scope: Procurement division. Dashboard generation — no causal analysis required.
4-step plan. No CCE required. Schema retrieval → DT resolve → layout select → render training-plan dashboard.

6 metrics retained. Plan summary metrics (goals/activities/employees/completions) + assign-level status distribution.
Rendering training-plan layout. Populating plan summary quad, by-status bar, donut, and assign details table.

summary.what: Procurement Division training plan contains 1 goal with 2 activities. 7 employees assigned. 3 fully completed (43%). 3 in progress at 50%. 1 overdue with 0% completion (Maria Mitchell).
summary.why: Plan launched May 10, 2016 as a cohort-style deployment — all 7 employees share the same start date. Due date is Dec 18, 2017. Overdue employee has shown no engagement activity since assign date.
summary.insight: With 3 completions, 3 in-progress, and 1 overdue, the plan is on track for most of the cohort. Maria Mitchell (0% completion, overdue) requires immediate manager escalation. The 50% completion rate for in-progress employees is still above the plan midpoint relative to the timeline.

outcomes.question: How many employees have completed the plan?
outcomes.answer: 3 of 7 (43%). Jane Goodall, Geraldine Seydoux, and Edmond Halley all at 100% completion.

outcomes.question: Who is at risk or overdue?
outcomes.answer: Maria Mitchell — 0% completion, overdue. Requires immediate manager escalation.

outcomes.question: What is the overall % completion rate?
outcomes.answer: 50% across all assigned activities. 3 fully complete, 3 in progress at 50%, 1 with 0%.

followup_suggestions: Send a nudge to the employees still in progress
Escalate Maria Mitchell's overdue status to her manager
Show me the Learner Summary for Edmond Halley
